

# 📘 **10.02 — Lab: Conditional Access & Zero Trust Policies**

*A hands‑on lab to build enterprise‑grade Conditional Access (CA) in Microsoft Entra ID.*

Conditional Access is the heart of Microsoft’s **Zero Trust** access strategy.\
Microsoft Learn defines CA as the policy engine that enforces access conditions based on user, device, location, risk, and session signals.    [\[forestall.io\]](https://www.forestall.io/resources/blog/how-to-secure-kerberos-authentication-protocol)

This lab teaches you **exactly how to build** a secure baseline CA configuration for any organization.

***

# 🎯 **Lab Goals**

By the end of this lab, you will:

### ✔ Build core Conditional Access policies:

*   Require MFA
*   Block legacy authentication
*   Require compliant/Hybrid Joined device
*   Require trusted locations
*   Protect admin roles
*   Enforce modern auth only

### ✔ Test, validate, and monitor policies

### ✔ Implement Zero Trust access principles

Microsoft states that the Defender/Entra identity architecture enforces Zero Trust through strong authentication and Conditional Access.    [\[forestall.io\]](https://www.forestall.io/resources/blog/how-to-secure-kerberos-authentication-protocol)

***

# 🧪 **Lab Prerequisites**

### ✔ Microsoft Entra ID P1 or P2

### ✔ MFA enabled

### ✔ At least one test user

### ✔ Azure AD Joined OR Hybrid Azure AD Joined device

### ✔ At least one registered authentication method

### ✔ Global Administrator or Security Administrator role

***

# 🏗 **1. High-Level Conditional Access Architecture**

![Visualization](data:image/png;base64,)

Conditional Access determines **if** access should be allowed based on security signals.    [\[forestall.io\]](https://www.forestall.io/resources/blog/how-to-secure-kerberos-authentication-protocol)

***

# 🛠 **2. Step-by-Step: Create a Conditional Access Policy**

Navigate to:

    Microsoft Entra admin center  
    → Protection  
    → Conditional Access  
    → Policies  
    → New policy

***

# 🔐 **3. Core Policy #1 — Require MFA for All Users**

### ✔ Create Policy

    Name: CA01 - Require MFA for All Users
    Assignments:
        Users: All users
        Exclude: Break-glass account
    Cloud apps: All cloud apps
    Conditions: None (default)
    Grant:
        Require multi-factor authentication

### 💡 Why it matters

Microsoft recommends MFA as the strongest authentication method and a core identity protection feature.    [\[forestall.io\]](https://www.forestall.io/resources/blog/how-to-secure-kerberos-authentication-protocol)

### ✔ Test

*   Login from new device
*   Verify MFA challenge and successful token issuance

***

# 🚫 **4. Core Policy #2 — Block Legacy Authentication**

Legacy protocols (POP, IMAP, SMTP Basic) bypass CA completely.

### ✔ Create Policy

    Name: CA02 - Block Legacy Authentication
    Users: All users
    Cloud apps: All cloud apps
    Conditions:
        Client apps: Other clients (legacy protocols)
    Grant:
        Block access

### 💡 Why

Legacy auth bypasses modern auth protections such as MFA and Conditional Access.    [\[learn.microsoft.com\]](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/identity/ad-ds/plan/dns-and-ad-ds)

***

# 🖥 **5. Core Policy #3 — Require Compliant or Hybrid Joined Device**

Ensures only trusted devices can access corporate apps.

### ✔ Create Policy

    Name: CA03 - Require Compliant or Hybrid Joined Device
    Users: All Users
    Cloud Apps: All cloud apps
    Conditions:
        Device Platforms: Windows (or all platforms)
    Grant:
        Require device to be marked as compliant
        OR
        Require Hybrid Azure AD joined device

### 💡 Why

Entra ID uses device state signals for secure access decisions.    [\[forestall.io\]](https://www.forestall.io/resources/blog/how-to-secure-kerberos-authentication-protocol)

***

# 🌍 **6. Core Policy #4 — Require Trusted Location for High-Risk Users**

Useful in regulated or sensitive environments.

### ✔ Create Policy

    Name: CA04 - High-Risk Users - Trusted Locations Only
    Users:
        Directory roles: Global Admin, Privileged Role Admin
        OR
        Users with risk = High
    Cloud Apps: All cloud apps
    Conditions:
        User risk: High
        Locations: Exclude named locations (e.g., Head Office)
    Grant:
        Require MFA
        Require compliant device
        OR block

### 💡 Why

Identity Protection's risky user detection helps enforce high-assurance authentication.    [\[dispersednet.com\]](https://www.dispersednet.com/active-directory/module5/replication-protocols.php)

***

# 🛡 **7. Core Policy #5 — Protect Admin Roles**

Admins must ALWAYS be under stricter CA controls.

### ✔ Create Policy

    Name: CA05 - Admin Protection
    Users: Directory roles → All privileged roles
    Cloud apps: All cloud apps
    Grant:
        Require MFA
        Require compliant device
        Require user risk = low

### 💡 Why

Microsoft recommends using Conditional Access to secure admin roles with strict access controls.    [\[forestall.io\]](https://www.forestall.io/resources/blog/how-to-secure-kerberos-authentication-protocol)

***

# 🔒 **8. Core Policy #6 — Require Modern Authentication Only**

Ensure cloud apps can only be accessed via OAuth/OIDC/SAML.

### ✔ Create Policy

    Name: CA06 - Modern Auth Enforcement
    Users: All users
    Cloud apps: All cloud apps
    Conditions:
        Client apps:
            - Select Browser
            - Select Mobile & Desktop apps
    Grant:
        Require MFA

### 💡 Why

Modern authentication protocols (OAuth2, OIDC, SAML) support Conditional Access, MFA, and Zero Trust.    [\[learn.microsoft.com\]](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/identity/ad-ds/plan/designing-the-site-topology)

***

# 🔍 **9. Step-by-Step: Test Policies**

### ✔ Use Test Accounts

*   “TestUser1” (standard)
*   “PrivAdmin1” (role)

### ✔ Validate in Sign-in Logs

Path:

    Entra admin center  
    → Protection  
    → Monitoring  
    → Sign-in logs

Check CA tab to see which policy applied or blocked access.

***

# 📊 **10. Continuous Access Evaluation (CAE)**

CAE revokes access *in real-time* when conditions change such as:

*   User password reset
*   User risk increases
*   Session revoked
*   MFA requirement triggered

CAE is part of Microsoft’s Zero Trust continuous verification model.    [\[forestall.io\]](https://www.forestall.io/resources/blog/how-to-secure-kerberos-authentication-protocol)

***

# ⚙️ **11. Automating Conditional Access Operations**

Microsoft Sentinel + Logic Apps + Graph API can automate:

### ✔ Bulk CA policy creation

### ✔ Policy deployment across tenants

### ✔ Real-time rollback for faulty policies

### ✔ Alerting on risky changes

### ✔ Backup CA policies

### ✔ Export CA policy configurations

Automation examples:

### Example 1 — Backup All CA Policies Daily (SOAR)

*   Trigger: Recurrence 24 hours
*   Action: Call Graph API `/identity/conditionalAccess/policies`
*   Store JSON in Storage or GitHub

### Example 2 — Auto-Disable Risky Policies

*   Trigger: Sentinel alert for “CA Policy Modified”
*   Action: Disable the policy via Graph API

### Example 3 — Auto-Notify SOC on Admin Policy Impact

*   Trigger: Privileged admin sign-in blocked
*   Action: Teams + Email alert to SOC
*   Action: Create ServiceNow ticket

***

# 🧵 **12. Mind Map**

    Conditional Access Lab
     ├─ Core Policies
     │   ├─ Require MFA
     │   ├─ Block Legacy Auth
     │   ├─ Require Compliant Device
     │   ├─ Protect Admin Roles
     │   ├─ Trusted Locations
     │   └─ Risk-Based Access
     ├─ Zero Trust
     │   ├─ Strong Auth
     │   ├─ Continuous Verification
     │   └─ Device + Identity + Risk
     ├─ Testing
     │   ├─ Sign-in Logs
     │   ├─ CA Insights
     └─ Automation
         ├─ Sentinel + SOAR
         ├─ Graph API
         └─ Backup & Rollback

***

# 🎯 **Summary**

In this lab, you configured:

✔ Modern Conditional Access\
✔ Zero Trust user and admin security\
✔ MFA enforcement\
✔ Legacy auth blocking\
✔ Device-based access controls\
✔ Risk-based access\
✔ CA automation through Sentinel/Graph

You now have the **baseline Conditional Access framework** used by enterprise security teams.

***
